---
title: "Project Report Template" # Give your report a title
course: "Advanced Analytics and Applications" # Specify the course name
team: "00" # Specify your team number
author:
  - name: Louis Schreier
    student-id: "7381065"
  - name: Daniel Schumacher
    student-id: "7409287"
  - name: Paola Ayelen Arenes Alonso
    student-id: "7453121"
  - name: Timo Ercole
    student-id: "7408459"
contact: student@smail.uni-koeln.de # Specify one contact for the project
supervisor: "Univ.-Prof. Dr. Wolfgang Ketter"
co-supervisor: "Janik Muires" # TA of the course is the co-supervisor
date: 2026-07-26 # Date of submission
---

<!-- While you could use \clearpage or \newpage, this is the recommended way to insert a page break in Quarto as it translates to the native page break command for the output format you are using (e.g., \clearpage for PDF, <div style="page-break-after: always;"></div> for HTML). -->
{{< pagebreak >}}

\pagenumbering{arabic}
\setcounter{page}{1}

{{< include sections/01-introduction.qmd >}}

{{< include sections/02-analysis.qmd >}}

# Citations and References
You can easily cite your sources using BibLaTeX. For example, you can cite the Quarto documentation [@quarto2024] or a textbook like "R for Data Science" [@wickham2023]. The bibliography will be automatically generated at the end of the document.

# Conclusion
The conclusion should summarize your findings and suggest future work.
